issue_title: BUG: cannot source deactivate env with conda install in it
issue_body:
Create an env with a name that begins with an underscore (i.e. _myenv) and install conda in the env.
source activate the env
try to source deactivate the env and conda won't deactivate back to the root env.

It appears that since conda is installed in the env conda thinks the env is the root env when it isn't.
They is no way to source deactivate back to the real root env.

issue_title: [Question] What data is in the third column of `conda list`
issue_body:
I've looked over the documentation and I can't figure out what the third column returned by `conda list` is:

```bash
conda list
...
svgwrite                  1.1.6                     <pip>
sympy                     0.7.5                    py27_0  
...
```

Presumably the entries with `<pip>` were installed via pip and not conda, but it isn't clear to me what `py27_0` means in the above example.

Is this information documented somewhere? If not, could you add it to `conda list -h`?

issue_title: TravisCI builds failing with latest Miniconda
issue_body:
We've been successfully using `http://repo.continuum.io/miniconda/Miniconda-latest-Linux-x86_64.sh` in our TravisCI builds for a few years now. Today we ran into issues on all of our builds that use Miniconda; see [this collection of builds](https://travis-ci.org/nengo/nengo/builds/318219757).

What seems to be happening is that the `conda install` command will exit out after a short time (the time is so short that it definitely is not something to do with TravisCI timing out). Here are a few of the failures:


issue_title: reindex doesn't preserve _version
issue_body: The reindex helper explicitly selects fields=('_source', '_parent', '_routing', '_timestamp') when preparing to scan the source index, which results in _version numbers getting reset. In my particular case we're using external versioning where losing the _version has some unpleasant side effects, but even with the default versioning I can't think of a reason this behavior would be desired given that the versioning is purely to help detect conflicts, not to enable change tracking.

issue_title: "timeout" in Doc-string for Elasticsearch.bulk should be request_timeout
issue_body:
`es.bulk(body=someBody,timeout=30)`
returns:
`elasticsearch.exceptions.RequestError: TransportError(400, 'parse_exception', 'Failed to parse setting [timeout] with value [30] as a time value: unit is missing or unrecognized')`

While:
`es.bulk(body=someBody,request_timeout=30)`
runs like a charm. 

So I think master/elasticsearch/client/__init__.py at line 772 should be:
_:arg request_timeout: Explicit operation timeout_
instead of:
_:arg timeout: Explicit operation timeout_

Two additional things:
1) I am new to this repository stuff, if this should have been a pull request instead of an issue, I am sorry
2) I am using elasticsearch 2.0, I assumed that in the last version it would be the same

issue_body:
### Description
**Outline the issue here:**

Deploying a connection between two gateways in two different RG in different regions fails when `--vnet-gateway2` is not provided as full resource ID and the message error is non informative

Command:
```bash
az network vpn-connection create -g PREMISE-RG -n PremiseToCloudConnection --vnet-gateway1 PREMISE-GATEWAY  --vnet-gateway2 CLOUD-GATEWAY --shared-key superk3y
```

Error:
```json
Deployment failed. {
  "status": "Failed",
  "error": {
    "code": "ResourceDeploymentFailure",
    "message": "The resource operation completed with terminal provisioning state 'Failed'.",
    "details": [
      {
        "code": "InternalServerError",
        "message": "An error occurred.",
        "details": []
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

But if I provide full Resource ID to `vnet-gateway2`, it actually works:
```bash
 az network vpn-connection create -g PREMISE-RG -n PremiseToCloudConnection --vnet-gateway1 PREMISE-GATEWAY  --vnet-gateway2 /subscriptions/xxxxx-xxx-xxx/resourceGroups/CLOUD-RG/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworkGateways/CLOUD-GATEWAY --shared-key superk3y
```

issue_title: BlobService class blobs with "question marks" in the path issue
issue_body:
Moved from https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-python/issues/494

Hi guys,

We are using Azure storage with one of our clients, and we are using the python sdk.

When we yield over the container_name with list_blobs method we get blobs with "question marks".
 After that if we try to get the same blob thru the get_blob_to_file (or get_blob) method,
 We get "Not found (The specified blob does not exist.)" exception.

I see that the issue is in the "_update_request_uri_query" method.
 You guys expect a query string after "question marks" but you allowing blobs with "question marks" in the path...

issue_title: Proposal for two micro-optimzations in RequestHandler.start function
issue_body:
Basically, I would like to work on two different micro-optimizations to speed up a bit, arround 5% and 10%, for the most costly function `RequestHandler.start`. I would like to gather your feedback before start working on them.

1) Handle most of the keepalive logic outside of the `RequestHandler.start`, and leave there only the none happy path - when the keep alive has to be canceled. This will save us to execute a few opcodes and many time syscalls. [1]

2) Get rid of the `set_tcp_cork` and `set_tcp_nodelay` operations and provide an API to be used by the internal layer that guarantees that a code path that touches any TCP configuration sets back the original values, this might be implemented using a context. [2]

I know that I might be missing something that would avoid implementing this two micro-optimizations, hence please feedback will be more than appreciate



[1] https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/aiohttp/web_protocol.py#L476
[2] https://github.com/aio-libs/aiohttp/blob/master/aiohttp/web_protocol.py#L426

issue_title: rename Connection.wait_closed()
issue_body:
`asyncio.AbstractServer` uses the following idiom:
- `close()` closes connection asynchronously, it's regular function.
- `wait_closed()` is a coroutine that waits for actual closing.

`Connection.wait_closed()` has different behavior: it sends disconnection signal to server and after that closes connection.

I guess  rename it to `.ensure_closed()`.

issue_title: iopub rate limits are too low by default, for visualization-heavy projects
issue_body:
I'm getting errors like:

```
IOPub data rate exceeded.
The notebook server will temporarily stop sending output
to the client in order to avoid crashing it.
To change this limit, set the config variable
`--NotebookApp.iopub_data_rate_limit`.
```

for just about every notebook I run, all of which have various visualizations.  From that message I had no idea what units the rate limits might be expressed in, but by adding enough zeros I eventually figured out that the problems go away if I launch jupyter with:

```
jupyter notebook --NotebookApp.iopub_data_rate_limit=10000000000
```

As mentioned in #1821, it is not obvious what limits are reasonable to set for this value by default, but I would like to put in my vote that the current value is at least an order of magnitude too low.  A sample matplotlib script that usually causes the problem is below, but I mostly use HoloViews, where I see the problem for just about every visualization when using the Bokeh backend, which requires a lot of JavaScript to be sent to the browser.  

As someone who in part maintains HoloViews, I now have to recommend that all of our users start Jupyter in this way, which makes it very awkward for them, for what I think is a very reasonable way to be using a Jupyter notebook for visualizations.  So I would strongly plead with you to increase this value by default so that it is only reached for actual error cases, not for ordinary visualization workflows.


```
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

freq = 100 
def sine(x, phase=0, freq=freq):
    return np.sin((freq * x + phase))

dist = np.linspace(-0.5,0.5,256)
x,y = np.meshgrid(dist, dist)
grid = (x**2+y**2)

testpattern = sine(grid, freq=freq)
methods = [None, 'none', 'nearest', 'bilinear', 'bicubic', 'spline16',
           'spline36', 'hanning', 'hamming', 'hermite', 'kaiser', 'quadric',
           'catrom', 'gaussian', 'bessel', 'mitchell', 'sinc', 'lanczos']

fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 6, figsize=(96, 48))
fig.subplots_adjust(hspace=0.3, wspace=0.05)

for ax, interp_method in zip(axes.flat, methods):
    ax.imshow(testpattern , interpolation=interp_method, cmap='gray')
    ax.set_title(interp_method)

plt.show()
```

issue_title: Confused about what Edit/View does
issue_body:
I am doing UI/UX testing for issue #2142. The Edit/View button are either not working or working in a way that is really confusing. Here is what I observe:

* For notebook, text files (non-html), clicking on the name of the file in the file browser opens the file in the corresponding editor (notebook, file editor).
* For those files the "Edit" button appears, but does the same thing. All notebook users have gotten used to just clicking on these files, so presenting different way of doing that same thing is confusing and makes it more difficult to find the other buttons they want to use. The button based UI is getting unwieldy with too many buttons that are context dependent. Not suggesting we move away from buttons now, but let's not show ones that are not needed.
* For HTML files, clicking on the file opens it in the text, editor, clicking the "Edit" button does the same thing. Again, the "Edit" button seems unnecessary. However, if you click "View" you get a new page with a nasty error:

![screen shot 2017-02-19 at 11 20 32 am](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/27600/23105062/5d1533f8-f696-11e6-8b22-674b60187508.png)

Is this a bug?

* For image files, clicking on the file opens it in View mode. The "View" button also shows and does the same thing. In this case, the "View" button is redundant. Furthermore, the "Edit" button shows, but errors after trying to open the file in the text editor. 

My recommendations:

* Remove the "Edit" and "View" buttons in situations where clicking the file already does the right thing.
* The only usage case that I see where that isn't the case is the "View" on HTML files. I think that makes sense to keep, but we should debug it.

@gnestor

issue_body:
Hi guys,
I am seeing errors when using vstack / hstack vs. using concatenate to assemble matrices. The below code block reproduces this.

Thanks once again for the awesome library :+1:

```
from autograd import numpy as np
import autograd

def vstack_test(matrix):
    stacked = np.vstack([matrix, matrix + 1])
    stacked = np.hstack([stacked, stacked + 1])
    dummy_left = np.ones(stacked.shape[0])
    dummy_right = np.ones(stacked.shape[1])
    
    stacked_inv = np.linalg.inv(stacked)
    return np.dot(np.dot(dummy_left, stacked_inv), dummy_right)

def concat_test(matrix):
    stacked = np.concatenate([matrix, matrix + 1])
    stacked = np.concatenate([stacked, stacked + 1], axis=1)
    dummy_left = np.ones(stacked.shape[0])
    dummy_right = np.ones(stacked.shape[1])
    
    stacked_inv = np.linalg.inv(stacked)
    return np.dot(np.dot(dummy_left, stacked_inv), dummy_right)

mat = np.random.normal(size=(2,2))
print vstack_test(mat)
print concat_test(mat)
print autograd.grad(concat_test)(mat)
 # next line throws an error, but the function should be equivalent to the previous line
print autograd.grad(vstack_test)(mat)
```

issue_title: How to convert defgrad to 1.2?

So, I guess I've done this conversion somehow incorrectly. I couldn't find any information on how to convert `defgrad` to the new `defvjp` (I only found this guide about switching from old `defvjp` to new `defvjp`: https://github.com/HIPS/autograd/blob/master/docs/updateguide.md#gradient-checking).

So, if I had a function:
```

def f(x):

def df(y, x):

        d = ...
        return d
    return g

f.defgrad(df)
```

I just changed `defgrad` method to `defvjp` function, nothing else changed:

issue_title: Redirect androlyze output to file
Coverage for installed packages + smarter reports
issue_body:
@schlamar I don't know if this could be of any use to anyone and I didn't open a pull request since it'd require small changes to both `pytest-cov` and `cov-core` repos. I found myself hacking around the coverage reporting behaviour manually all the time (like changing current folder to location of the package to avoid ugly paths) so I wondered if it could be done upstream.

If you run coverage on an installed package (e.g. via `pytest --pyargs`), or more generally on a package that's not in your current folder, your coverage report would look super ugly like so:

```
Name                                                                 Stmts   Miss  Cover   Missing
------------------------------------------------------
/home/smirnov/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/arrow/__init__        4      0   100%
/home/smirnov/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/arrow/api            13      0   100%
/home/smirnov/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/arrow/arrow         295      0   100%
/home/smirnov/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/arrow/factory        58      0   100%
/home/smirnov/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/arrow/formatter      78      0   100%
/home/smirnov/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/arrow/locales       371      2    99%   1182, 1189
/home/smirnov/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/arrow/parser        172      0   100%
/home/smirnov/anaconda/lib/python2.7/site-packages/arrow/util            7      0   100%
------------------------------------------------------
TOTAL                      998      2    99%
```

The same will go to html/xml reports.

Wouldn't it would be nice to have reports like this instead?

```
Name        Stmts   Miss  Cover   Missing
--------------------------------------------
__init__        4      0   100%
api            13      0   100%
arrow         295      0   100%
factory        58      0   100%
formatter      78      0   100%
locales       371      2    99%   1182, 1189
parser        172      0   100%
util            7      0   100%
--------------------------------------------
TOTAL                      998      2    99%
```

Would you consider adding something like a `--compact-report` option to `pytest-cov` which would then pass some flag to `CovController.summary()` so it can do something like this before generating reports:

``` python
prefix = os.path.commonprefix(cov.data.measured_files())
if prefix:
    cov.file_locator.relative_dir = prefix  # +9000 nice points for all reports
```

That's pretty much the entire implementation :)

I could open pull requests to `cov-core` and `pytest-cov` if you'd want.

Thanks.

issue_title: RFE: Support token auth for non-admin users
issue_body:
This is a request for enhancement to support token-based authentication for users that don't have the administrator role.

Suppose I create a partition "bobspartition" and a user "bob".  If I configure bob's authorization like this:

```
admin@(ip-1-2-3-4)(cfg-sync Standalone)(Active)(/Common)(tmos)# list auth user bob
auth user bob {
    description bob
    encrypted-password $6$LsSnHp7J$AIJ2IC8kS.YDrrn/sH6BsxQ...
    partition Common
    partition-access {
        bobspartition {
            role operator
        }
    }
    shell tmsh
}
```

then I *can* do "ssh bob@1.2.3.4", log in, and create/modify/delete objects like virtual servers in /bobspartition using tmsh.  But if I use the iControl REST API, I cannot.  Even if I specify the "partition=" object when init'ing the BigIP object.  I hacked around inside of the f5-icontrol package to ensure the URL I was POSTing to was mgmt/tm/ltm/pool/~bobspartition .  Below is a backtrace with the URL (removed IPs):

```
    logging.debug(post_message)
icontrol.session.iControlUnexpectedHTTPError: 401 Unexpected Error: F5 Authorization Required for uri: https://1.2.3.4/
mgmt/tm/ltm/pool/~bobspartition
Text: u'{"code":401,"message":"Authorization failed: user=https://localhost/mgmt/shared/authz/users/bob resource=/mgmt/tm/lt
m/pool/~bobspartition verb=GET uri:http://localhost:8100/mgmt/tm/ltm/pool/~bobspartition referrer:4.3.2.1 sender:4.3.2.1","referer":"4.3.2.1","restOperationId":32471,"errorStack":["java.lang.SecurityException: Authorization failed

on.RestServer$1.run(RestServer.java:271)","at java.util.concurrent.Executors$RunnableAdapter.call(Executors.java:471)","at j
ava.util.concurrent.FutureTask.run(FutureTask.java:262)","at java.util.concurrent.ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor$ScheduledFutur
eTask.access$201(ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor.java:178)","at java.util.concurrent.ScheduledThreadPoolExecutor$ScheduledFuture

```

If I flip bob's admin credential on, all of a sudden this same query works.  Here's an authorization config for "bob" that works (all that changed is bobspartition/operator -> all-partitions/admin):

```
auth user bob {
    description bob
    encrypted-password $6$LsSnHp7J$AIJ2IC8kS.YDrrn/sH6BsxQgmm...

    partition-access {
        all-partitions {
            role admin
        }
    }
```

I talked to some folks who work on the control plane, and they say that if you want to use the REST API as a user who isn't the administrator, you must use token-based authentication. What I would like is a new option when constructing a BigIP object in f5-common-python that lets me ask for token-based auth.  Then I'd like the API to transparently get a token on my behalf (using username and password I provided) and store it in memory.  Whenever I make a request, it should use that token.

issue_title: Workaround the condition exclude(any_char_field='')
issue_body:
Never use the condition **`exclude(any_char_field='')`** for `CharField(null=True)`. This condition works only with fields with the attribute `null=False`.
Use `filter(any_text_field__gt='')` (that is an equivalent in all databases) 
or `exclude(any_text_field=None)` (that is the equivalent only in SFDC)

The same problem is with TextField, with all operators and also with a negated `Q(filter)` function: 
  `exclude(char_field__in=('', 'a'))` excludes only `'a'` 
  `exclude(char_field__lt='b'))` excludes only `'' < x < 'b'` 
  `filter(~Q(char_field=''))` is the same as exclude(char_field='') `

This problem is not related to fields that can never contain an empty string: numbers, booleans, dates, foreign keys etc. It is related only to fields that can save and keep both possible values `None` or `''` in Django. 

**Explanation:**
It is not a bug in django-salesforce, but a result of an incompatitility between ways, how Django and SFDC work with empty strings.

SFDC uses a strange logic `'' = null` for text fields, which is very problematic in negated SOQL  conditions:

* The `null` value is saved as empty string `''` to text fields.  
* SOQL evaluates WHERE conditions so that the empty string equals to both `null` and `''`
* The problem is that the negated filter `exclude(field=value)` in Django is equivalent to `NOT (field = value AND field IS NOT NULL)` if the field is defined with the attribute `null=True` in the django.db model, which is usual for a default Salesforce text field..

In the case of empty string, a queryset with `exclude(any_text_field='"")` is compiled to `NOT (field = '' AND field IS NOT NULL)` which is True in SOQL for all records, including the empty strings. It is equivalent to `.all()` in Django query set.
 
**Solution:**
The solution would be to use modified models with `null=False` for CharField and TextField. Also `inspectdb` should be modified accordingly.

issue_title: Sub task: Tablet display
Implement commenting system
Add support for app-shells/rc
issue_body:
This is the reimplementation of the Plan 9 shell which may be found in main portage tree and in some other Linux distributions. Since the original Plan 9 shell does not have `prompt` function powerline cannot support it. Some notes:

1. Rc has equivalent for `$pipestatus` from zsh which is `$status`, but it may contain not only numeric statuses, but also signal names and `+core`: e.g.

        ; echo 'main() {puts(0);}' | tcc -run - ; echo $status

    will produce

        0 sigsegv+core

    or

        0 sigsegv

    . This provides information that makes sense to display, but it must be tested and powerline should be modified to work with this information.
2. `prompt` variable is a list where second item is a continuation prompt.
3. It should be explicitly stated that powerline is not compatible with Plan 9 rc.

issue_title: why don't spotify player status in MacOS be prefered to use osascript?
issue_body:
in /powerline/segments/common/players.py line 374
```
if 'dbus' in globals() or not sys.platform.startswith('darwin'):
```
this means that even in macOS dbus is preffered way.
but in macOS dbus-python is very buggy, dbus module is buggy no matter installed by brew or ports.
on the contrary, osascript is preinstalled on OS X systems and rarely have issue.

I think that line should be something like
```
if 'dbus' in globals() and not sys.platform.startswith('darwin'):
```

or even just
```
if not sys.platform.startswith('darwin'):
```
because linux system has to use dbus anyways.

issue_title: Consider including Python unit tests in packaged wheel
issue_body:
Most open source Python packages include the test code in the wheel for distribution. There seem to be a lot of upsides to this, and only a few downsides. Upsides:

* Wheels are testable anywhere, without having to copy any other files over. Tests can be run more easily on a range of environments, just by having the wheel installed.
* Whether the test code uses package-level or relative/local imports, it'll work, since it will always be located relative to the package code where it expects to be.
* Various other scripts in our repo that move test code around and run the tests can be simplified.

Downsides:

* Would increase the size of the wheel by about 2 MB (surprisingly substantial - this includes some in-repo test data).
* Would increase the footprint of Turi Create installations.

issue_title: The attrib process does not handle a `: ` embedded in a .ABOUT file text field
issue_body:
The `attrib` process has a problem when it reads a .ABOUT file and encounters a free-form text field that looks like this: 
```
notes: Three files (they may be build files) have the following copyright: Copyright (c) 1991-2014 Altera Corporation.  See, e.g., alpha/source/core/rng/trng/toolruns/quartus/terasic_de0_nano/trng.qpf (which also refers to "the terms and conditions of the Altera Program License Subscription Agreement, Altera MegaCore Function License Agreement, or other applicable license agreement . . . ").  A fourth file has a bsd-simplified license and this copyright: Copyright (c) 2015, NORDUnet.
```
In this case this `notes` field was written by the `gen` process to the .ABOUT file from the original .csv input.  However, the `attrib` process is over-interpreting the various cases of  `: ` (a colon followed by a space) as an attempt to state some kind of additional mapped field, as seen in this error message. 

```
CRITICAL: alpha/source/core/rng/trng.ABOUT: Cannot load invalid ABOUT file: '/var/folders/yd/730fpwcj0wx3zljndw2phcrr0000gn/T/tmp34sld4s6/alpha/alpha/source/core/rng/trng.ABOUT': ScannerError(None, None, 'mapping values are not allowed here', <yaml.error.Mark object at 0x10f013470>)
mapping values are not allowed here
  in "<unicode string>", line 4, column 74:
     ... es) have the following copyright: Copyright (c) 1991-2014 Altera ... 
                                         ^
```   
So it seems that either (1) attrib needs to figure out how to handle this case, or (2) gen needs to put some kind of escape values around the colon+space so that it is not interpreted as another yaml key:value by attrib or (3) some other solution. 

test files attached.

[test-data-and-results.zip](https://github.com/nexB/aboutcode-toolkit/files/1968308/test-data-and-results.zip)

issue_title: Weird bug in known_positives retrieval
issue_body:
I have a dataset of user and products as a pandas dataframe, and change it to a sparse matrix (in this example, I create some custom dummy-data to reproduce the bug):

```python
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from lightfm import LightFM
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split

raw_data = pd.DataFrame(columns=['USER', 'PRODUCTS'],
                        data=[
                        ['Jo','Apple'],
                        ['Jo','Orange'],
                        ['Zette','Apple'],
                        ['Zette','Banana'],
                        ['Zette','Banana'],
                        ['Jocko','Orange'],
                        ['Jocko','Orange'],
                        ['Jocko','Strawberry'],
                        ['Tintin','Apple'],
                        ['Tintin','Strawberry'],
                        ['Tintin','Strawberry'],
                        ['Tintin','Banana'],
                        ['Toto','Pineapple'],
                        ['Tata','Apple']])

# Custom pre-processing part
utility_matrix = pd.get_dummies(raw_data, columns=['PRODUCTS']).groupby('USER').sum()
utility_matrix[utility_matrix > 1] = 1

# train/test split (same size)
train_df, test_df = train_test_split(utility_matrix, test_size=0.5)

# pandas dataframe to coo matrix
train = train_df.to_sparse().to_coo().astype(np.int32)
test = test_df.to_sparse().to_coo().astype(np.int32)

item_labels = train_df.columns.values

data = {'train': train,
        'test': test,
        'item_labels': item_labels}
```

Then, I train the model following tutorial https://github.com/lyst/lightfm/blob/master/examples/quickstart/quickstart.ipynb

```python

model = LightFM(loss='warp')
model.fit(data['train'], epochs=30, num_threads=2)

from lightfm.evaluation import auc_score

train_auc = auc_score(model, train).mean()
test_auc = auc_score(model, test).mean()

print('AUC: train %.2f, test %.2f.' % (train_auc, test_auc))

from lightfm.evaluation import precision_at_k

print("Train precision: %.2f" % precision_at_k(model, data['train'], k=1).mean())
print("Test precision: %.2f" % precision_at_k(model, data['test'], k=1).mean())
```

The resulting evaluation scores are all 1 or NaNs...

```
AUC: train nan, test nan.
Train precision: 1.00
Test precision: 1.00
```

I looked at the recommendations to see what happens:

```python
def sample_recommendation(model, data, user_ids):


    n_users, n_items = data['train'].shape

    for user_id in user_ids:
        known_positives = data['item_labels'][data['train'].tocsr()[user_id].indices]

        scores = model.predict(user_id, np.arange(n_items))
        top_items = data['item_labels'][np.argsort(-scores)]

        print("User %s" % user_id)
        print("     Number of Known positives: %s" % len(known_positives))
        print("     Known positives:")

        for x in known_positives[:3]:
            print("        %s" % x)

        print("     Recommended:")

        for x in top_items[:3]:
            print("        %s" % x)

sample_recommendation(model, data, [0, 1])
```

outputs:

```
User 0
     Number of Known positives: 5
     Known positives:
        PRODUCTS_Apple
        PRODUCTS_Banana
        PRODUCTS_Orange
     Recommended:
        PRODUCTS_Banana
        PRODUCTS_Orange
        PRODUCTS_Pineapple
User 1
     Number of Known positives: 5
     Known positives:
        PRODUCTS_Apple
        PRODUCTS_Banana
        PRODUCTS_Orange
     Recommended:
        PRODUCTS_Banana
        PRODUCTS_Apple
        PRODUCTS_Pineapple
```

I figured out that `known_positives = data['item_labels'][data['train'].tocsr()[user_id].indices]`  always retrieves all the products.

So I tried with an example to verify this:

```python
In [31]: data['train'].tocsr()[1].A
Out[31]: array([[ 1.,  0.,  0.,  0.,  0.]], dtype=float32)

In [32]: data['train'].tocsr()[1].indices
Out[32]: array([0, 1, 2, 3, 4], dtype=int32)
```

As far as I understand from the movielens examples, `data['train'].tocsr()[1].indices` should return the indices of positives values. But here for some reason, it returned every index.

I finally overcomed this issue by doing:

```python
In [33]: np.nonzero(data['train'].tocsr()[1].A)[1]
Out[33]: array([0])
```

I updated the `sample_recommendation` function with `known_positives = data['item_labels'][np.nonzero(data['train'].tocsr()[user_id].A)[1]]` and it worked well.

However, my evaluation functions `auc_score` and `precision_at_k` are still returning perfect scores and I feel it could be because of the wrong retrieval of `known_positives`? Or I did something wrong?

issue_title: Artefact in Ineichen clearsky model for sun close to horizon
issue_body:
Consider following example:
```python
import pandas as pd
import pvlib
times = pd.date_range('2018-02-16', '2018-02-17', freq='min')
location = pvlib.location.Location(latitude=60., longitude=0.)
for model in ['ineichen', 'haurwitz', 'simplified_solis']:
    location.get_clearsky(times, model=model)['ghi'].plot(label=model, legend=True)
```
which results in following figure:
![pvlib_clearsky](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1150402/36589304-28988d7e-188b-11e8-98ac-5ca098fe9312.png)

The (default) 'ineichen' clearsky model seems erratic at sunrise and sunset. The effect is especially visible for high latitudes during Winter when the sun slowly approaches the horizon.

Upon closer look in the code, I found the following comment:
https://github.com/pvlib/pvlib-python/blob/04bd7532f54b550313d84309c3d3d19818694ccc/pvlib/clearsky.py#L98-L103
which refers to the formula:
https://github.com/pvlib/pvlib-python/blob/04bd7532f54b550313d84309c3d3d19818694ccc/pvlib/clearsky.py#L122-L126

However, apart from the typo mentioned, there is another important difference between both formula of Solar Energy 73, and that's the `np.exp(0.01*airmass_absolute**1.8)` factor, which is only present in the formula of pg 311, but not in the formula of pg 156!

It seems like the artefact is exactly a result of that `np.exp(0.01*airmass_absolute**1.8)` factor. This factor slowly increases from 1.01 at solar zenith to 1.95 at a zenith angle of 85°, but then shoots to 1000 when the sun reaches the horizon.

So, maybe there was an error also in the formula of Solar Energy 73, pg 311, and that factor wasn't supposed to be there?

Nevertheless, in reference [3] (Reno et al., 2012), this model was found to excel for US sites with the same formula as used by pvlib. The US sites were all below the [49th parallel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/49th_parallel_north). I wonder whether this model is still recommendable for higher latitudes?

issue_title: Shouldn't download if compression isn't used.
issue_body:
I submitted a support ticket but wanted to raise the issue here, as well. We are making a request for a PDF be compressed using the following code:

```
# Initiate the compression via Cloud Convert
process = cc_api.createProcess({
    "mode": "compress",
})

process.start({
    "mode": "compress",
    "input": "download",
    "file": self.pdf_file.url,
})

process.wait()

path = ...code to generate path to file...
process.download(path)
```

Sometimes, the `process.wait()` will receive a 200 response from the API with what appears to be a finished compression and all of the required attributes to generate a download. However, in some cases, there's `message` property in the JSON response that reads: `We could not compress this file. It seems this file is already compressed.`

When the `process.download(path)` is fired from `self.api.rawCall("GET", ...)` in process.py the request to download the file is returned a 404 response with a message of `File already deleted. Use the 'save' parameter, otherwise files will be deleted after the first download.`.

In my mind, it doesn't make sense for the compression API to return a 200 if the download is not going to work. For now, I've modified our code to look for the `message` above but I feel like the original API response should be more explicit if the file is not going to be available.

issue_body:
I am sorry if this was already discussed but I am new as a Z3 user. I am using Z3 to theorem prove first-order formulae stating properties of the real numbers. I am not doing anything in particular except declaring the formula in which every symbol used is declared as Real. The first-order theory of the real numbers is decidable as it is isomorphic to the real closed field [Tarski 1951] for which many decision procedures have been proposed, at least two with double exponential elementary complexity [Monk 1974, Collins 1975] and one with exponential space or parallel exponential time elementary complexity [Ben-Or 1986]. Based on this, I do not understand why Z3 responds Unknown when it could take a lot, but answer Sat or Unsat. 

Best regards and thanks.

issue_title: CALLLESS simuvex state option making up successors
issue_body:
The CALLLESS state option is set for the call_state at the first address of a function.  During execution I am looking at the path successors in order to determine if the path is in a loop or not (checking if it was already in the address trace).  Since the CALLLESS option overwrites all function calls, the path successors does not include the function addr.  However, this is an issue when it comes to a tail call at the end of a function.  Looking at the path successors it states that the next possible address (immediately following the function call) is a successor even though it isn't.  The CFG however does the analysis correctly and lists the function call as the only successor.

An example is for coreutils/true.c version 8.26 function: usage
In the CFG you can see in the bottom right corner the end of usage at address 0x4016fa.  There is no successor other than the function call (overwritten using CALLLESS during execution).  However, during analysis angr thinks that 0x401701 is a successor of 0x4016fa.

![cfg](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/25495067/30704807-d5179296-9ec1-11e7-8029-36be03d21986.png)

issue_title: angr failed to solve constraints for arm branch instruction such as bxlo,ldrlo, etc.
Bug in paged_memory.py when deep-copying pages for write
issue_body:
Hi! While analyzing a binary, I ran into an issue of uninitialized memory access (which resulted in a new symbolic variable, which lead to a state explosion). I tracked down the issue to the management of page copies within the machine state. Even though new objects are created for the machine state and the memory, `SimPagedMemory.branch()` uses `new_pages = dict(self._pages)`, which creates shallow copies of the memory pages. This is fine for reads, but the pages need to be deep-copied for writes to avoid overlaps in machine states. As far as I understood, in `SimPagedMemory._get_page()`, if a write is requested, the class checks whether the page in question has been already deep-copied (the page number is listed in `SimPagedMemory._cowed`). If no, than a deep copy is created of the page, and the page number is added to `SimPagedMemory._cowed` to avoid further deep copies.

The VEX IR for the block which triggers the bug: [vex_ir.txt](https://github.com/angr/angr/files/1371297/vex_ir.txt). This IR has 6 successors (of which only 1 will be followed, the jump to `0x24dc0`), and the relevant instructions for the bug are the following:

```
   11 | if (t42) { PUT(ip) = 0x24db0; Ijk_Boring }
   <...>
   30 | if (t59) { PUT(ip) = 0x24db4; Ijk_Boring }
   <...>
   41 | STle(t68) = t51
   <...>
   58 | if (t90) { PUT(ip) = 0x24dc0; Ijk_Boring }
   <...>
   73 | if (t105) { PUT(ip) = 0x24dc4; Ijk_Boring }
   <...>
   77 | STle(t108) = t93
   <...>
   79 | STle(t110) = t95
   <...>
   81 | STle(t112) = t97
   <...>
   107 | if (t139) { PUT(ip) = 0x24dec; Ijk_Boring }
   NEXT: PUT(pc) = 0x00024dd4; Ijk_Boring
}
```

At lines 11 and 30, two successor states are generated from the original, their memory pages are shallow copies of the original's. At line 41, the first write in the block happens, so the original state makes a deep copy of the page in question and adds the page number to `SimPagedMemory._cowed`. At lines 58 and 73, two additional successor states are generated, with shallow copies to the original's memory pages. The bug is triggered at line 77: this write request references the same memory page that has already been deep copied at line 41. Because the page number has already been added to `SimPagedMemory._cowed`, no deep copy is created, the memory page is written to. Even though two successor states (lines 58 and 73) hold shallow copies of the that same memory page.

The issue should be solved by emptying `SimPagedMemory._cowed` whenever a copy is made of the `SimPagedMemory` instance. That should be in `SimPagedMemory.branch()`, but the current version of the code doesn't have this instruction.

issue_title: Symbolic accesses and endianness
issue_body:
When using angr with this script:

```
import angr
import claripy

endness_values = [None, 'Iend_LE', 'Iend_BE']

for endness in endness_values:

    print "Testing symbolic load/write with endness=" + str(endness)

    binary = '/bin/ls' # we don't care for the binary
    project = angr.Project(binary, load_options={'auto_load_libs' : False})

    state = project.factory.entry_state(remove_options={angr.options.LAZY_SOLVES}, 
    	add_options={angr.options.SYMBOLIC_WRITE_ADDRESSES}) # store range => 128, load range => 1024 

    # if you add this, then when endness='Iend_LE', angr returns the expected results
    # state.memory.endness = endness

    endness=None #

    # addr in [0, 1]
    addr = claripy.BVS('addr', 64)
    state.se.add(addr >= 0)
    state.se.add(addr < 2)

    val = 0x01020304

    state.memory.store(addr, val, 4, endness=endness)   # we write 0x01020304 at addr
    res = state.se.any_n_int(state.memory.load(addr, 4, endness=endness), 10) # we should read 0x01020304 at addr

    print "Expected: " + hex(val)
    for r in res:
        print "Result: " + hex(r)
    print
```

Output (angr master branch):
```
Testing symbolic load/write with endness=None
Expected: 0x1020304
Result: 0x4010203L
Result: 0x2030401L

Testing symbolic load/write with endness=Iend_LE
Expected: 0x1020304
Result: 0x4010203L
Result: 0x2030401L

Testing symbolic load/write with endness=Iend_BE
Expected: 0x1020304
Result: 0x4010203L
Result: 0x2030401L
```
This is wrong: `res` should be `[val]`

If we uncomment the line `state.memory.endness = endness`, we make progress: the output for `Iend_LE` is correct (the others are still wrong):
```
Testing symbolic load/write with endness=None
Expected: 0x1020304
Result: 0x4010203L
Result: 0x2030401L

Testing symbolic load/write with endness=Iend_LE
Expected: 0x1020304
Result: 0x1020304

Testing symbolic load/write with endness=Iend_BE
Expected: 0x1020304
Result: 0x4010203L
Result: 0x2030401L
```

If we use latest release of angr on pip, the result is completely wrong:
```
Testing symbolic load/write with endness=None
Expected: 0x1020304
Result: 0x101a000L
Result: 0x1010000L
Result: 0x101a001L
Result: 0x1000001L
Result: 0x10da001L
Result: 0x1020304
Result: 0x1000000L
Result: 0x10fa001L
Result: 0x1012000L
Result: 0x105a001L

Testing symbolic load/write with endness=Iend_LE
Expected: 0x1020304
Result: 0x80018004L
Result: 0x8001a404L
Result: 0x1020304
Result: 0x8001a004L
Result: 0x4L
Result: 0x2018004L
Result: 0x8001e404L
Result: 0x82018004L
Result: 0x8001e004L
Result: 0x8011e004L

Testing symbolic load/write with endness=Iend_BE
Expected: 0x1020304
Result: 0x1810000L
Result: 0x1010000L
Result: 0x1000001L
Result: 0x1c10002L
Result: 0x1d10402L
Result: 0x1020304
Result: 0x1000000L
Result: 0x1c10402L
Result: 0x1c10000L
Result: 0x1d10c02L
```
This is nonsense since the commit 3d033e0f1739008dbd86910a27becc28d52225d8 is not yet included and it is needed to get some (almost) correct results.

Hi,

This is more a question than a defect. When I "python setup.py install" other modules than pyresample, it is not rare to obtain a ".egg-info" file installed under site-packages/. This is not the case for pyresample:

issue_title: How to ensure we're not between 2 intervals
issue_body:
Hi, this is not an issue, more like a discussion, not sure if this is the right medium, you can close this if you don't feel this belongs here.

So i'm using this tool for an integration test on an authentication system.  Since this is time based and the intervals are 30 seconds... if a test fires just at the wrong time, it's gonna fail caus' the code is not right.

So i'm wondering how you guys deal with this.

1. Do you have a server that accepts the current and last code so it's a bit more elastic on token acceptance?

2. Do you just relaunch your tests when they fail?

3. Do you use some waiting mechanism if the interval is approaching such as this:
```
import datetime
import time
import pyotp


class SafeTOTP(pyotp.TOTP):
    def __init__(self, *args, **kwargs):
        self.time_buffer_to_refresh = kwargs.pop('time_buffer_to_refresh', 2)
        super().__init__(*args, **kwargs)

    def now(self):
        time_to_next_refresh = self.interval - datetime.datetime.now().timestamp() % self.interval
        if time_to_next_refresh < self.time_buffer_to_refresh:
            time.sleep(time_to_next_refresh)
        return super().now()
``` 

Thanks for the tool by the way! :)

issue_title: planning for v0.4.0 release
issue_body:
It looks like we have a nice set of additions that could make up a new release in the near future, although there are a few outstanding PRs that would be nice to have.  Any objections to planning a v0.4 release prior to the end of the year?

Here is a summary of the key things that have been completed since v0.3.  Let me know if I missed anything.

PRs already merged:
--------------------------
**new features**
- inverse 1D and 2D stationary wavelet transforms (iswt, iswt2) were added (#93)
- much faster n-dimensional forward and inverse discrete wavelet transforms. (#104)
- Complex data type support in all wavelet transforms. (#99)

**maintenance**
- substantial cleanup/refactoring of the C code (#59, #102)
- Matlab testing was updated to use python-matlab-bridge rather than mlabwrap (#100)

**bugs fixed**
- float32 inputs were not always respected.  All transforms now return float32 outputs when called using float32 inputs. (#106)


PRs in progress/review:
-------------------------------
**new features**
- add wavedecn and waverecn functions (#67)
- add axis argument to dwt / dwt2 / dwtn  (#116, #120)

**maintenance**
- API cleanup (more descriptive function names, etc) (#55)
- ? break up _pywt.pyx as suggested in #127

**bugs fixed**
- incorrect detail coefficients returned by downcoef when level > 1 (#98)

**breaking changes**
- disallow mismatching coefficient lengths in idwt2 (#125)

issue_title: Possible to store credstash variables in more than one DynamoDB instance?

Is there a work around for this?

Thanks,
Marissa

issue_title: rs-software-device example doesn't track bytes_per_pixel properly
issue_body:
| Required Info                         |                                                                |
|---------------------------------|------------------------------------------- |
| Camera Model                       | D415 | 
| Firmware Version                   | 05.08.15.00) | 
| Operating System & Version |   Ubuntu 16  | 
| Kernel Version (Linux Only)    |                                        | 
| Platform                                 | PC  |
| SDK Version                            | 2.10.2                     | 

While debugging my own issues, I noticed that get_bytes_per_pixel() gives 0 for synthetic frames. In the rs-software-device example, I modified the code a bit to the following to reproduce the issue:

```
if (auto as_depth = depth.as<rs2::depth_frame>()) {
  std::cout << as_depth.get_bytes_per_pixel() << std::endl;
  points = pc.calculate(as_depth);
}
```

I noticed that in the function on_video_frame(), if you multiply the input BPP by 8 you get the correct answer (which is 2). It's as if the input was expecting bits_per_pixel. The rs2_software_video_frame struct, which is the input to on_video_frame, has a data member bpp. The example uses this as if it were bytes_per_pixel, but does it actually mean bits_per_pixel?

issue_title: `--logger --log-path=.` are not actually producing a log file
Potential security issues with `get_ip()` changes in v4
issue_body:
I'm a little concerned by the change to use `ipware.ip.get_ip()` in v4.

1. There is no documentation to suggest that `django-ipware` may need to be configured.
2. There is now no documentation on the potential attack vector if the proxy doesn't set `X-Forwarded-For` (or whatever the first header used in `IPWARE_META_PRECEDENCE_ORDER` is) explicitly, c.f. [`USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST`](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/2.0/ref/settings/#std:setting-USE_X_FORWARDED_HOST)
3. ~~`django-ipware` says to use `get_ip()` if the server is **not** accessible on the Internet, see [here](https://github.com/un33k/django-ipware#how-to-use).~~ (Although looking at the code the distinction between `get_ip()` and `get_real_ip()`, the latter doesn't count private IP addresses as "real". What they really mean here is use `get_ip()` if there are also clients that may connect from an internal private network otherwise use `get_real_ip()`.)
4. Previously `django-axes` allowed configuring the maximum number of proxies in case of spoofed entries. This is no longer possible. `django-ipware` provides `get_trusted_ip()` but it isn't particularly useful in it's current form.

It would probably be wise to recommend setting `IPWARE_META_PRECEDENCE_ORDER` to `['HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR', 'REMOTE_ADDR']` or at least indicate that this was the original behaviour. Checking all of these headers by default feels risky.

Forgive me, but this feels rushed and ill thought through. It was working fine for me and issue #275 and pull request #280 fail to even give any evidence of a problem. Every comment in #275 is regarding `AttributeError` when looking up `META` on `NoneType` - `django-ipware` is still using `request.META`. This feels like a push to remove code in favour of a dependency for minor gain.

issue_title: Re-factor `make_catalog_backup`: implementar estructura de directorio abreviada alternativa a la actual
issue_body:
Actualmente se usa una estructura de directorio como la siguiente:

`/catalog/{catalog_id}/dataset/{dataset_id}/distribution/{distribution_id}/download/{distribution_filename}

Se propone agregar una opción para estructura abreviada `use_short_path=True` como la siguiente:

`/{catalog_id}/{dataset_id}/{distribution_identifier}.{distribution_format}`

En la versión acortada, el nombre del archivo de la distribución debe *ser único siempre* para no pisar eventualmente otra distribución. Para esto se usa el identifier de la distribución.

issue_title: Some devices registered successfully returned with APIException 400 RC instead of 202
issue_body:
Obvious bug here:

```
		elif status == 202:
			raise ibmiotf.APIException(400, "Some devices registered successfully", r.json())
```


```
	def registerDevices(self, listOfDevices):
		"""
		Register multiple new devices, each request can contain a maximum of 512KB.
		The response body will contain the generated authentication tokens for all devices.
		You must make sure to record these tokens when processing the response.
		We are not able to retrieve lost authentication tokens
		It accepts accepts a list of devices (List of Dictionary of Devices)
		In case of failure it throws APIException
		"""
		bulkAdd = ApiClient.bulkAddUrl % (self.host )
		r = requests.post(bulkAdd, auth = self.credentials, data = json.dumps(listOfDevices), headers = {'content-type': 'application/json'}, verify=self.verify)

		status = r.status_code

		if status == 201:
			self.logger.debug("Bulk registration successful")
			return r.json()
		elif status == 202:
			raise ibmiotf.APIException(400, "Some devices registered successfully", r.json())
		elif status == 400:
			raise ibmiotf.APIException(400, "Invalid request (No body, invalid JSON, unexpected key, bad value)", r.json())
		elif status == 403:
			raise ibmiotf.APIException(403, "Maximum number of devices exceeded", r.json())
		elif status == 413:
			raise ibmiotf.APIException(413, "Request content exceeds 512KB", r.json())
		elif status == 500:
			raise ibmiotf.APIException(500, "Unexpected error", None)
		else:
			raise ibmiotf.APIException(None, "Unexpected error", None)
````

issue_title: API changes for the logger in Flask 1.0.0+
issue_body:
When upgrading to FLASK v1.0.0+ I get this error:

```bash
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "PATH/lib/python3.6/site-packages/flask/_compat.py", line 35, in reraise
    raise value
  File "PATH/app.py", line 40, in <module>
    talisker.flask.register(app)
  File "PATH/lib/python3.6/site-packages/talisker/flask.py", line 62, in register
    app._logger = logging.getLogger(app.logger_name)
AttributeError: 'Flask' object has no attribute 'logger_name'
```

From [changelog](http://flask.pocoo.org/docs/1.0/changelog/):
> Flask.logger has been simplified. LOGGER_NAME and LOGGER_HANDLER_POLICY config was removed. The logger is always named flask.app. The level is only set on first access, it doesn’t check Flask.debug each time. Only one format is used, not different ones depending on Flask.debug. No handlers are removed, and a handler is only added if no handlers are already configured.

issue_title: Split "Supported software" into separate (more elaborate) pages
issue_body: The [Supported Software](https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/supported.html) page (`docs/supported.rst` in the git repo) contains too many sections. We should move almost all of these sections (certainly all sections that describe a server setup) to their own page in the [Other tutorials](https://vdirsyncer.pimutils.org/en/stable/tutorials/index.html) folder (`docs/tutorials/`) and add more details.

issue_title: improve error reporting
issue_body:
Using `cerbot -d foo`:

Actual results:
```
Obtaining a new certificate
An unexpected error occurred:
The request message was malformed :: DNS name does not have enough labels
Please see the logfiles in ... for more details.
```

Expected results:
1. Tell me the problematic domain (certbot does when it reports errors, but not when it gets an error from boulder)
1. Ideally, I should get a single message for a single problem, not two possible errors for the same problem (see below for an actual instance of this)
1. It should be possible for certbot to report errors to users in a language other than English (this means that boulder should be reporting errors by identifier + message instead of just message)
1. Explain the problem in English (not RFC-speak)
* In English, that means "request message was malformed" should go
* If there's a problem, the explanation should also be in English, so instead of "not enough labels", hint at how many labels are required (currently it's hard coded to 2 -- but, based on what boulder could know from publicsuffix, I think this could theoretically be dynamic)

I'm not sure if certbot can report the problematic domain.
The `labels` bit comes from boulder.
The malformed bit comes from certbot.

This whole process is spread across two to four repositories (certbot/certbot, certbot/website, letsencrypt/boulder, letsencrypt/website). There's no good place for me to manage this process.

I'm happy to provide replacement text and even do the PRs. 

For reference:
```
$ certbot -d foo -d b-r -d  -d a...a -d a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a
Requested domain  is not a FQDN because label  is too long.
```

```
$ certbot -d foo -d b-r -d a...a -d a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a
```

```
a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.

Obtaining a new certificate
An unexpected error occurred:
The request message was malformed :: Invalid character in DNS name
```

```
$ certbot -d a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a
Obtaining a new certificate
An unexpected error occurred:
The request message was malformed :: DNS name too long
```

```
$ certbot -d a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a
Obtaining a new certificate
An unexpected error occurred:
The request message was malformed :: DNS name has too many labels
```

Personally, my preference is to be told about all errors at once.
One of the most annoying experiences I have when picking a password is being told "password too short" only to be told later "doesn't have a number" and later "needs a symbol" and then "needs a capital letter".

I'm not a strickler on that point.

But, the fact that both the client and server are trying to handle some of the same errors (`too long`) is a bit odd.
If reporting won't be "all problems", I'd personally favor reporting the error along the critical path. For my:
`a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a.a`
item, that would be "too may levels" as opposed to  "too long", because even if I shortened it, I would still get too many levels (as shown above).

issue_title: Remove major, easy to fix warts from release script
nginx + separate ssl/non-ssl blocks
Make parser.py: add_server_directives documentation consistent with functionality
issue_body:
Documentation and at least one calling function assume that add_server_directives performs: "Add or replace directives in the first server block with names."

Instead, it add or replaces directives in all server blocks with names

issue_title: Dual solution after recalculation
issue_body:
After I resolve a problem the dual values of a constraint is not updated, i.e. take a look at the following code: 

    import pyscipopt as scp
    Mod=scp.Model()
    Mod.setMaximize()
    Mod.setPresolve(scp.SCIP_PARAMSETTING.OFF)
    Mod.hideOutput()
    x=Mod.addVar(ub=3,name="x",obj=0.1,vtype="C")
    y=Mod.addVar(ub=3,name="y",obj=1)
    c=Mod.addCons(x+y<=3,"C1")
      
    Mod.optimize()
    print("SCIP")
    print("obj: ", Mod.getObjVal())
    print(x.name,Mod.getVal(x))
    print(y.name,Mod.getVal(y))
    print("pi: ",Mod.getDualsolLinear(c))    
    Mod.freeTransform() 
    Mod.chgRhs(c,2)
    
    Mod.optimize()
    print("obj: ", Mod.getObjVal())
    print(x.name,Mod.getVal(x))
    print(y.name,Mod.getVal(y))
    print("pi: ",Mod.getDualsolLinear(c))    

Output:

    SCIP
    obj:  3.0
    x 0.0
    y 3.0
    pi:  0.1
    obj:  2.0 
    x 0.0
    y 2.0
    pi:  0.1

But clearly `pi` should be 1 after the constraint is tightened. If I also remove `x` from the objective function I receive the following: `Warning: no dual solution available for constraint C1`

Is this a bug or do I need to call some update function? I can just use another LP solver, just curious why I observe this behavior.

Thanks for all the help guys :)

issue_title: Scour inserts inappropriate <g> element when scouring Inkscape multi-line text.
issue_body:
When the  "group collapsing" option is unchecked, and the "create groups for similar attributes" is checked, Scour inserts a <g> element inside the <text> element when adjacent <tspan>s share the same properties. This prevents the rendering of the <tspan> elements.

See https://bugs.launchpad.net/inkscape/+bug/1516538

Scour should either leave the <tspan>s alone or if all <tspan>s in a <text> element share the same properties, put the properties on the <text> element.

issue_title: post_generation objects can't be overridden with a computed "pre" value
Feature request: set_latest
issue_body:
Parameters roughly speaking work like this:

- To set the parameter value via instrument communication, use `set`
- To get the parameter value via instrument communication, use `get`
- To get the parameter value without instrument communication, use `get_latest`
- To set the parameter value without instrument communication, use... `_save_val`?

Shouldn't that be `set_latest`? Furthermore, `_save_val` currently does not quite work as expected in cases where a `Parameter` has no `get_cmd`, but does have a `val_mapping`. An example:

```python
p1 = Parameter('p1', val_mapping={'one': 1, 'two': 2}, set_cmd=lambda x: x)
p1._save_val('one')
p1.get()
```
Raises a TypeError, because the `get` calls a `lambda: self.raw_value`, but the `raw_value` is `None`, since `_save_val` does not update the `raw_value` if there is a `val_mapping`. Link to code:
https://github.com/QCoDeS/Qcodes/blob/b68979ef084a19373976daaef1d6a19ba5644ae9/qcodes/instrument/parameter.py#L341-L355

Ideally, I would like
```python
p1 = Parameter('p1', val_mapping={'one': 1, 'two': 2}, set_cmd=lambda x: x)
p1.set_latest('one')
```
to set `p1._latest` to `{'value': 'one', 'ts': datetime(blah blah), 'raw_value': 1}`. Similarly for cases with scaling, offset, and parsers.

@QCoDeS/core 

PS: This came up in #1218, so although slightly contrived, in **can** happen in the real world.

issue_title: avgpool stride confusion in ResNet-50
issue_body:
Hello,

The default value `None` of `stride` for avgpool methods imposes a stride which is equal to `kernel_size` leading to textual representations like below:

```
(avgpool): AvgPool2d (size=7, stride=7, padding=0, ceil_mode=False, count_include_pad=True)
```

Although a stride of 7 or 1 in this case does not change anything in the output (since the input tensor's spatial resolution is 7x7), I had confusion when comparing PyTorch architecture to [caffe proto file](https://github.com/KaimingHe/deep-residual-networks/blob/master/prototxt/ResNet-50-deploy.prototxt#L2299) where the stride is explicitly given as 1. So I think it would be better to explicitly pass `stride=1` ResNet's last `avgpool` to make things clear. What do you think?

issue_title: Make Ray API thread safe
issue_body:
The purpose of this issue to track the efforts of making Ray API fully thread safe.

# Existing problems
Although currently it's not allowed for users to call Ray API from non-main threads, there're also some internal background threads in the Python worker, which causes thread-safety issues.

1) One example is that the local scheduler client is used in 3 threads (main thread, import thread, and flush-profile-data thread). And the access of its underlying connection isn't always protected (see https://github.com/ray-project/ray/pull/2306#pullrequestreview-135707250 for more detailed explanation). 
2) Access of the buffer for profile data isn't protected.
3) worker.redis_client is also used in multiple threads. (Not sure if it's thread-safe, can anyone help confirm?)

# Todos
Besides fixing the existing problems, we'd also like to make Ray API fully thread-safe. It'll allow users to run multi-thread programs on ray. Here's the list of what needs to be done:
- [x] Fix problem 1 by making the underlying c++ code thread-safe. (#2386)
- [x] Fix problem 2 by protecting the buffer with a lock
- [x] Lock other clients and stateful operations in the worker (#2248)

# Java
TBD

# Related PRs
#2248
#2349
#2386

cc @alexbao

issue_title: Tags: 'final' and 'finalfinal'
issue_body:
Hi team,

Thanks for your project. We added [Pyt](https://linuxsecurity.expert/tools/pyt/) to our security tools section. While reviewing we noticed we could only extract tags from the GitHub repo, as no releases are used. Now tags are sorted by alphabet, meaning that the tagged version 'finalfinal' always is listed as the highest release number. Not sure if it is possible, but you could re-tag it 'final' and 'finalfinal'. Then the community can pick up future released and promote your project. Otherwise, it gets stuck on a very old version ;-)

issue_title: package names
issue_body:
Let's clean up the package names to be consistent:

Proposal:

* `jupyter-widgets-schema` -> `@jupyter-widgets/schema`
* `@jupyter-widgets/htmlmanager` -> `@jupyter-widgets/html-manager`
* `widgetsnbextension` is a private package, but for consistency we could call it `@jupyter-widgets/notebook-manager`
* Should we move the jupyterlab manager into widgets? `@jupyterlab/nbwidgets` -> `@jupyter-widgets/jupyterlab-manager`?

issue_title: trio.open_file() doc confusion
issue_body:
It seems trio.open_file() must be used with `async with await ...` rather than `async with ...`.  (Why is that-- can it be improved?  Yury Selivanov calls it an antipattern at https://github.com/python/asyncio/issues/316#issuecomment-183075661)

There is an example in the docs which omits `await`:

```
* Async file objects can be used as async iterators to iterate over
     the lines of the file::

        async with trio.open_file(...) as f:
            async for line in f:
                print(line)
```

https://github.com/python-trio/trio/blame/94a2f8bd082d99a53626309e221da2b348f8c2d7/docs/source/reference-io.rst#L614-L616

    from sentinelsat import SentinelAPI, read_geojson, geojson_to_wkt

Change tensorflow to tensorflow-gpu in requirements.txt
issue_body:
Currently, if you already have tensorflow-gpu installed, after you install studioml, it will install and start using the cpu version of tensorflow instead. However if you then uninstall tensorflow (cpu version) it will complain the following when you launch studio:

```
studio-ui
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/studio-ui", line 4, in <module>
    __import__('pkg_resources').require('studioml==0.0.0')
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3049, in <module>
    @_call_aside
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3033, in _call_aside
    f(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 3062, in _initialize_master_working_set
    working_set = WorkingSet._build_master()
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 658, in _build_master
    ws.require(__requires__)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 972, in require
    needed = self.resolve(parse_requirements(requirements))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python2.7/dist-packages/pkg_resources/__init__.py", line 858, in resolve
```
